mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, prompting risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during supply-driven unemployment spikes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand contraction, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when at least one series shows month-over-month growth, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of positive industrial production growth, rising factory output signals strengthening corporate earnings capacity, which generates positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <45% of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and triggering central bank tightening expectations that suppress equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive when real rates are negative, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of anchored inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and higher aggregate consumption, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in <50% of months with payroll growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of labor productivity declines.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.5%, economic contraction triggers recession fears and margin compression, causing immediate negative equity price adjustments over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of low-GDP months, or the signal loses significance when GDP declines are driven by temporary inventory adjustments rather than demand shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures and capacity constraints, which force monetary tightening and generate negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is supply-side driven, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.
